macro_segments.title: Introduction and Opening Case: The Shomeret Yavam with Inherited Property (Nichsei Milug)

micro_segments.title: Setup: Rachel as Shomeret Yavam with Inherited Property Rights
The Question: Why Does Shimon's Uncertain Claim Not Defeat Rachel's Certain Possession When She Is Alive
The Rule: Ein Safek Motzi Midei Vadai - Uncertainty Cannot Defeat Certainty of Possession
Contrasting Case: House Collapse on Minor and Father - When Beit Shammai Permits Splitting by Safek
Explanation: Shtar She-Omed Ligvot Kegavuy Dami - A Document Ready to Collect Is Like Already Collected

macro_segments.title: The Sotah Case: Woman Suspected of Adultery and the Ketubah When Husband Dies Before She Drinks

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: Sotah Whose Husband Dies Before She Drinks the Waters - Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel on Ketubah Collection
Interpretation: Sotah as Safek (Doubtful Status) Collecting from Heirs Who Have Vadai (Certain) Possession
Proof from Sotah: Shtar Doctrine Explains Why Woman Can Collect Full Ketubah Despite Safek Status
Why Not Ask from Sotah Case Instead of House Collapse Case
Answer: Kesuvas Isha (Woman's Ketubah) Might Be Special Due to Chinah (Attractiveness) - Not Necessarily a General Rule

macro_segments.title: The Ketubah Question in the Mishnah: Does Beit Shammai Split the Ketubah When Rachel Dies

micro_segments.title: The Problem: Mishnah Speaks of Splitting Property but Is Silent on Ketubah Division
Rava's Resolution: Beit Shammai Concedes Ketubah Stays with Shimon—Reading the Mishnah Carefully
Language Analysis: Yachloku Yorshei Ha-Ba'al Im Yorshei Ha-Av Indicates Father's Family in Possession
Conclusion: Beit Shammai Admits Ketubah Remains with Shimon; Agrees to Ein Safek Motzi

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Alternative Explanation: Property Acquired During Marriage to First Husband vs. After His Death

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Framework: Reisha Is When She Inherited After First Husband Died; Seifa Is When She Inherited While Married to First Husband
The Logic: When She Inherited After First Husband Died, She Has Undisputed Title; When Married, Husband's Position Complicates Her Ownership
Why Split in Seifa: First Husband Had Strong Position; When He Dies, Shimon Takes Over as Co-Equal Partner, Not Weaker Partner
The Assumption: Yadav Keyidah - Husband and Wife Are Equal in Rights During Marriage (Beit Hillel Position)

macro_segments.title: Rava's Rejoinder: Husband's Position Is Actually Stronger; The Real Question Is About Maamar

micro_segments.title: Rava Agrees with Abaye's Framework but Disagrees on the Facts: Husband's Position Is Always Stronger, Not Equal
The Real Difference: Reisha Is When No Maamar Done; Seifa Is When Maamar Done with Shimon
Maamar's Effect: Maamar Makes Her ודאי Arusah and Safek Nisu'ah, Which Is Why They Split

macro_segments.title: The Dispute Over Maamar's Implications: Rabbi Elazar's Qualification on Maamar's Scope

micro_segments.title: The Attribution: Rabbi Elazar Follows Rava's Position; Rabbi Yose Bar Rabbi Chanina Follows Abaye's Position
Challenge to Rabbi Elazar: Did He Not Say Maamar Only Works to Push Away Tzarot, Not for Property Rights
Rabbi Elazar's Clarification: Maamar Creates Both Arusa Status and Safek Nisu'ah; My Point Was About Chalitzah Still Being Needed

macro_segments.title: Support from Mishnah Language: Nichasim Ha-Nichnasim Ve-Ha-Yotzim Imah Supports Abaye's Reading

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Argument: The Phrase 'Property That Comes In and Goes Out With Her' Proves Seifa Concerns Property Acquired While Married
The Problem Remains: Why Does Mishnah Emphasize 'When She Dies' If Real Difference Is When She Inherited
Resolution: Despite This Problem, Language of Coming and Going Supports Abaye's Reading Overall

macro_segments.title: After Yibum: Full Wife Status and Ketubah Collection Rights

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: Once Shimon Does Yibum, Rachel Becomes His Full Wife
Consequence 1: Divorce via Get Only, Not Chalitzah Anymore (No Longer Yevamah Status)
Consequence 2: Can Remarry Him with Kiddushin if She Marries Another First
Challenge: Why Emphasize Get Divorce Unless Worried She Remains Yevamah Despite Yibum
Answer: Mishnah Teaches That Yibum Completely Changes Her Status; She Is No Longer Sister-in-Law, Just Wife
Ketubah Rules: Collects from First Husband's Estate; If Insufficient, from Second Husband's Estate for Protection

macro_segments.title: The Primary Obligation: The Mitzvah of Yibum Is on the Oldest Brother

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: Mitzvah Begadol Leyabem - The Duty of Yibum Primarily Falls on the Oldest Brother
Procedure if Oldest Refuses: Go to Other Brothers to See If Any Will Do Yibum
If All Refuse: Return to Oldest and Compel Him to Do Either Yibum or Chalitzah
Exceptions to Waiting: Don't Wait for Younger Brother to Mature, Oldest to Return, or Incapacitated Brother to Recover

macro_segments.title: The Dispute: Is Yibum of Younger Brother Preferable to Chalitzah of Older Brother

micro_segments.title: The Case: Younger Brother Wants Yibum; Older Brother Wants Chalitzah - Which Takes Precedence
Opinion 1 (Rabbi Yochanan): Biat Katan Adifa - Yibum of Younger Brother Is Preferable Because Yibum Is Greater Mitzvah
Opinion 2 (Rabbi Yehoshua Ben Levi): Chalitzat Gadol Adifa - Older Brother Has Primary Obligation; His Action Takes Precedence
The Logic of Each Opinion: Yibum Focuses on Best Mitzvah; Chalitzah Focuses on Who Bears the Obligation

macro_segments.title: Reading the Mishnah: What Does 'Lo Ratza' Mean in Light of the Dispute

micro_segments.title: First Reading: If 'Lo Ratza' Means Refuses to Do Yibum but Willing to Do Chalitzah, Mishnah Proves Yibum Preferable
Gemara's Correction: 'Lo Ratza' Means Refuses to Do Anything - Both Yibum and Chalitzah
Consequence: If All Brothers Refuse Everything, We Must Force Someone; Why Force the Oldest
Answer: Even with Force, the Mitzvah Remains Primarily on Oldest; Force Him Because He's the Obligated Party

macro_segments.title: Application to Waiting Cases: Why Don't We Wait for Younger to Mature or Oldest to Return

micro_segments.title: The Question: If Yibum Is Preferable, Why Not Wait for Younger Brother to Mature to Do Yibum
Parallel Question: If Oldest Has Primary Obligation, Why Not Wait for Oldest to Return from Overseas
Both Questions Answered: We Don't Wait in Either Case Because Mitzvah Cannot Be Delayed
The Priority: Getting the Mitzvah Done Now Trumps Finding the Optimal Person to Do It

macro_segments.title: Secondary Dispute: When Both Must Do Chalitzah, Is Older Brother's Chalitzah Preferable to Younger

micro_segments.title: New Version of Dispute: Chalitzat Katan vs. Chalitzat Gadol - Is Mitzvah Begadol Still Apply to Chalitzah
Opinion 1: Chalitzat Gadol Adifa - Mitzvah Begadol Applies Even to Chalitzah; Older Brother Should Do It
Opinion 2: Ki Hadadi Ninhu - Once in Chalitzah Context, They're Equivalent; No Preference by Age
Reading the Mishnah: 'Lo Ratzu' Means All Refuse Everything; We Return to Oldest; This Proves Preference for Oldest Even in Chalitzah
Gemara's Correction Again: Actually Means All Refuse Everything; We Still Don't Know Answer to Chalitzah Preference from Mishnah Alone
